title: machine: Refactor Ready socket flow to be more generic
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

Implement generic `CreateReadySock`, `DeleteReadySock`, and `ListenReadySock` functions for the `VirtProvider` interface. The intent of this is to enforce a more generic flow for the ready socket across the providers (except WSL which does not use a ready socket). 

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *jakecorrenti* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [jakecorrenti] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Would love to get some suggestions for the `ListenReadySocket` design. The fact that the different providers use different functions, which in turn have different parameters and return types, resulted in a less than ideal function signature

@Luap99 I agree with everything you said. 

My only question is how does having a separate interface solve the issue of how HyperV doesn't represent a ready socket the same way as AppleHV and QEMU? That is really the source of all this complexity in my eyes

IMO: the methods should probably accept no arguments. IMO, you want three functions:

`CreateReadySocket() error`
`WaitForReady() error`
`TeardownReadySocket() error`

Any information necessary for this to work should be put in the config file, in the provider-specific bits. No `interface{}` as it's all in the config and not an argument.
